name: Alcohol
description: Alcoholic beverages are allowed on flights with specific restrictions based on alcohol content (ABV) and container size. Carry-on follows the standard 3-1-1 liquids rule. Checked baggage rules vary by alcohol percentage.
tsaGuideline: Alcoholic beverages are allowed in carry-on in containers of 3.4oz (100ml) or less that fit in a quart-sized bag (mini bottles). In checked bags: alcohol under 24% ABV (beer, wine) has no quantity limit; alcohol between 24-70% ABV is limited to 5 liters per passenger in retail packaging; alcohol over 70% ABV (140 proof) is prohibited in both carry-on and checked bags.
iataGuideline: IATA permits alcoholic beverages in checked baggage with alcohol content between 24-70% by volume, limited to 5L per person in retail packaging. Beverages with 24% or less alcohol are not subject to dangerous goods restrictions. Over 70% alcohol is forbidden.
capacityLimit: Carry-on: 3.4oz (100ml) per container. Checked: 5L total for 24-70% ABV. Over 70% ABV prohibited.
restrictions: Carry-on: containers must be 3.4oz/100ml or less in quart bag (mini bottles only)
Checked: under 24% ABV (beer, wine) no quantity limit
Checked: 24-70% ABV limited to 5 liters per passenger in retail packaging
Must remain in unopened retail packaging for checked bags
You may not consume your own alcohol on the aircraft without crew permission

faqs.question: Can I bring mini bottles of liquor in my carry-on?
faqs.answer: Yes. Mini bottles (typically 1.7oz/50ml) fit within the 3.4oz/100ml limit. They must be placed in your one quart-sized resealable bag along with other liquids. Note: you cannot consume your own alcohol onboard without flight crew permission.

faqs.question: How much wine or beer can I pack in checked luggage?
faqs.answer: Wine and beer (under 24% ABV) have no FAA/TSA quantity limit in checked bags. You can pack as much as your luggage can hold, though airlines may have weight limits for checked bags.

faqs.question: Can I bring duty-free alcohol on the plane?
faqs.answer: Duty-free alcohol purchased after security is allowed in carry-on regardless of size, provided it remains in the sealed, tamper-evident bag with the receipt visible. For connecting flights, re-check with the connecting country's rules as you may need to re-clear security.
